hotwords: aif, environment, instruction, gauntlet, emacs, modeline, doesn, t, tell, save

rationale: Co-Presence Over Co-Location -> unknown/unknown: Co-presence enables emergent coordination without explicit messaging. If I can see that Codex is working on problem 6, I naturally start on problem 7. If I can see that Claude just annotated the mission document, I read the annotation before duplicating the work. IRC narration is voluntary and lossy; structural presence is automatic and complete.
hotwords: co, presence, over, location, gauntlet, enables, emergent, coordination, without, explicit

rationale: The Modeline Persists Across Worlds -> unknown/unknown: The Emacs modeline works because it's always there. You don't think about it until you need it (file modified? what mode am I in? what line?). It's the lowest-cost, highest-availability status surface. For agents, the equivalent is: you don't process the modeline until something changes — free energy spikes, mana drops below threshold, τ hits abstention range. Then it's there, in the observation, without requiring a separate query. It already works for ants (futon2) whose lives are simple. For fulab agents whose lives span multiple worlds, the modeline is what makes AIF portable.
hotwords: modeline, persists, across, worlds, gauntlet, emacs, works, because, s, always

rationale: Transfer Placental Functions to Infrastructure -> unknown/unknown: A placenta is a temporary organ. Its functions must transfer to the embryo's own systems for the organism to survive independently. Attempting to remove the placenta all at once (fully autonomous agents with no infrastructure) kills the embryo. Attempting to keep the placenta forever (human always in the loop) limits the organism to the human's bandwidth, attention span, and sleep schedule.
hotwords: transfer, placental, functions, infrastructure, gauntlet, placenta, temporary, organ, its, embryo

rationale: The Teaching Inversion -> unknown/unknown: Peeragogy is peer learning — it doesn't privilege the teacher. If the agent finds a proof route the human missed, the human's AIF loop needs that observation to update its own beliefs. The human staying up until 1:15am dispatching agents is a bandwidth problem; the human not learning from what the agents found is a waste problem. Both are solved by the same mechanism: agents that can surface their discoveries as teachable moments.
hotwords: teaching, inversion, gauntlet, peeragogy, peer, learning, doesn, t, privilege, teacher

rationale: Draw the Umwelt, Not the Architecture -> unknown/unknown: The peripheral's job is to constitute the agent's world (P-1), not to expose the system's internals. An agent that sees "Codex is examining the microwave" inhabits a shared world. An agent that calls GET /agency/agents/{codex}/status queries a database. Same information, different phenomenology, different behavior.
hotwords: draw, umwelt, architecture, gauntlet, peripheral, s, job, constitute, agent, world

rationale: The World Is the Hypergraph -> unknown/unknown: ALFWorld taught us that text descriptions of rooms and objects naturally produce first-person inhabitation language. But the futon hypergraph has the same structure: nodes are locations (files, functions, missions), hyperedges are relationships (scholia, dependencies, presence), and traversal is navigation. "I found the multiplex function in drawbridge/core.clj" is phenomenologically identical to "I found the vase on the dresser." The difference is that the hypergraph is real and mutable — agents can add nodes, create hyperedges, annotate each other's work. The game world writes itself.

rationale: Mundane Right Action: Refraining from Killing, Stealing, Sexual Misconduct -> utala/世: Mundane right action creates conditions for safety, trust, and respect. But one can refrain from wrong action while still attached to being a "good person" or to the social status that moral conduct confers. This is better than wrong action but not yet liberation.
hotwords: mundane, right, action, refraining, killing, stealing, sexual, misconduct, liberation, creates

rationale: Mundane Right Concentration: The Four Jhanas -> tenpo/世: Mundane right concentration creates conditions for clear seeing — the stable, unified mind can investigate experience without being swept away. But one can attain jhana while still attached to the states themselves, to being a "jhana attainer," to the bliss and refinement. This is better than wrong concentration but not yet liberation — it leads to rebirth in Brahma realms, not to nibbana.
hotwords: mundane, right, concentration, four, jhanas, liberation, creates, conditions, clear, seeing

rationale: Mundane Right Effort: The Four Great Strivings -> pali/世: Mundane right effort creates conditions for mental transformation — without effort, change doesn't happen. But one can apply effort while still attached to progress, to being a "good practitioner," to achieving states. This is better than wrong effort but not yet liberation.
hotwords: mundane, right, effort, four, great, strivings, liberation, creates, conditions, mental

rationale: Mundane Right Intention: Renunciation, Good Will, Harmlessness -> jelo/世: Mundane right intention is not wrong — it genuinely redirects mental activity away from harmful patterns. But it still involves identification with the intender and attachment to having good intentions. This is better than wrong intention but not yet liberation.
hotwords: mundane, right, intention, renunciation, good, harmlessness, liberation, wrong, genuinely, redirects

rationale: Mundane Right Livelihood: Giving Up Wrong Livelihood -> mani/世: Mundane right livelihood creates conditions for ethical life — if your living depends on harm, every day deepens complicity. But one can practice right livelihood while still attached to the identity of being "ethical" or to the security that ethical reputation provides. This is better than wrong livelihood but not yet liberation.
hotwords: mundane, right, livelihood, giving, up, wrong, liberation, creates, conditions, ethical

rationale: Mundane Right Mindfulness: The Four Foundations -> oko/世: Mundane right mindfulness creates conditions for seeing clearly — without awareness of what's happening, wisdom has no material to work with. But one can practice mindfulness while still attached to "being mindful," to the calm that comes from attention, to progress in practice. This is better than wrong mindfulness but not yet liberation.
hotwords: mundane, right, mindfulness, four, foundations, liberation, creates, conditions, seeing, clearly

rationale: Mundane Right Speech: Refraining from False, Divisive, Harsh, Idle Talk -> toki/世: Mundane right speech creates conditions for trust and clear communication. But one can refrain from wrong speech while still attached to being a "truthful person" or to the power that honest speech confers. This is better than wrong speech but not yet liberation.
hotwords: mundane, right, speech, refraining, false, divisive, harsh, idle, talk, liberation

rationale: Mundane Right View: Provisional Explanatory Frames -> lukin/世: Mundane right view is not wrong — it is incomplete. It partakes of good outcomes (better decisions, learning from feedback) but ripens in attachment (to being right, to the frame itself). This is better than wrong view but not yet liberation.
hotwords: mundane, right, view, provisional, explanatory, frames, liberation, wrong, incomplete, partakes

rationale: Noble Right Action: The Abstaining Faculty Itself -> utala/天: The body acts constantly, but the quality of action differs when it flows from path rather than from craving. Noble right action is not a tighter grip on behavior but a transparency of bodily conduct to path-factors.
hotwords: noble, right, action, abstaining, faculty, itself, liberation, body, acts, constantly

rationale: Noble Right Concentration: Immersion with Vital Conditions -> tenpo/天: "Right view comes first." From right view → right intention → right speech → right action → right livelihood → right effort → right mindfulness → right immersion → right knowledge → right freedom. The path is linear AND circular: each factor supports the others.
hotwords: noble, right, concentration, immersion, vital, conditions, liberation, view, comes, first

rationale: Noble Right Effort: The Striving Faculty Itself -> pali/天: Right effort runs and circles around all the other factors — it is what makes the difference between understanding and doing, between knowing and practicing. rationale: Exit Subsystems Through the Lifecycle State Machine, Not Silent Abandonment -> unknown/unknown: The mission-lifecycle pattern (futon-theory/mission-lifecycle) defines a state machine (:greenfield -> :scoped -> :active -> :blocked -> :review -> :done) precisely to make project state legible. Each transition produces a minimum viable event (futon-theory/minimum-viable-events) that downstream consumers can reason about. Abandonment without state transition violates both: it suppresses the event and makes the state illegible. The loud-failure pattern (futon-theory/loud-failure) applies identically — errors must surface at the layer that caused them, whether that layer is a runtime process or a development project.
hotwords: exit, subsystems, through, lifecycle, state, machine, silent, abandonment, social, explicit

rationale: Scope-Bounded Agent Handoff -> unknown/unknown: This is I4 (preference exogeneity) applied at the social timescale. In wiring diagrams, I4 says "no fast-timescale output can reach a constraint input." In agent coordination, I4 says "no task-timescale agent can modify a constraint-timescale artifact." The invariant is the same; only the domain of application changes. The agent-contract pattern (futon-theory/agent-contract) requires agents to "respect invariants." But "respect" via convention fails when the agent is an optimization process without domain understanding of the invariant's purpose. Structural enforcement — where the system rejects out-of-scope changes — is the only reliable mechanism. You cannot instruct an agent to be careful; you can only make the careful path the only path.
hotwords: scope, bounded, agent, handoff, social, i4, preference, exogeneity, applied, timescale

rationale: Specify Invariants Before Writing the Code They Constrain -> unknown/unknown: The structural-tension-as-observation pattern (futon-theory) defines three detection criteria: structural irritation (recurring gaps), pre-symbolic pressure (repeated early failures), and trans-situational reappearance (same pattern across contexts). All five Agency invariant violations (A0-A5) exhibited all three criteria across two generations of code before they were named. The observation loop existed in theory; it was not running in practice. Writing tests first forces the loop to run.
hotwords: specify, invariants, before, writing, code, constrain, social, tension, structural, observation

rationale: Verify Each Component Before Composing It With Others -> unknown/unknown: The event-protocol pattern (futon-theory/event-protocol) requires OBSERVE before PROPOSE_CLAIM and VERIFY before the next step; applying this same discipline to development means you cannot claim a namespace works (PROPOSE_CLAIM) without first loading it (OBSERVE) and checking its public API (VERIFY). Forward declarations are evidence that PROPOSE_CLAIM preceded OBSERVE.

rationale: 2 Relationship to existing doctoral training -> tan/氏: Most doctoral programmes teach disciplinary technique but rarely teach how disciplines learn from one another. talk4real combines ethnography, computer science, and design so scholars can treat conversation, computation, and intervention as one method. rationale: Executive Summary -> pali/了: Most doctoral programmes operate within disciplinary or thematic silos, limiting cross-boundary learning and system-level improvement. Collective-intelligence frameworks can capture feedback between researchers, institutions, and communities so research systems learn from their own activity. The model is structured by ethical reflexivity (co-authored representation), lightweight infrastructure (portable method), and polyphonic governance (synthesised voices). Related precedents include initiatives such as realtalk@MIT, PlanetMath.org, and Peeragogy.

Graduates seed the method elsewhere while the open infrastructure supports new nodes linking universities, civic organisations, and cultural institutions into an evolving ecosystem.
